comments.body: This PR addresses only some of the format and layout issues. No changes to any content.
There will likely be more follow-up PRs but I prefer to do them in easier to digest hunks.

LGTM
Really nice scrubbing @vrothberg, TYVM!
Smoke tests aren't hip though.
Thanks, @TomSweeneyRedHat. I restarted the tests; again a timeout.

`$ podman ...`
Indicates you run this command as non root
`# podman ...` 
indicates you execute the command as root.

Does changing these would indicate we want people to run these commands as non root?

> Does changing these would indicate we want people to run these commands as non root?

I wasn't aware of that at all. But it doesn't seem to be used consistently as some cases where `$ sudo podma ...`. I would prefer to leave a note in the description of each command that it works rootless as well, which I consider to be more explicit and easier to understand.

FWIW, when I see '$' in a man page, I think I can run it as either non-root or root.  If there's a '#' there, then I'm thinking only root.  When I saw the changes that added the '$' I liked it as a subtle way to show that you can run them as non-root.
